aboutData.title: Hi There! I'm Zachary Navea
aboutData.subtitle: STEM Student
aboutData.text: I am 17 years old. I am a STEM student who is interested in coding and technology. I enjoy learning how things work, especially when it involves computers, programs, and logical thinking. Coding helps me practice creativity because I can create something useful from simple ideas. I also like problem solving because it challenges me to think carefully, analyze situations, and find the best solution.

services.title: Notebook
services.text: A notebook is a solid because it has a definite shape and volume. It has physical properties such as color, thickness, size, and texture. The paper inside it is also matter because it occupies space and has mass.

services.title: Pen
services.text: A pen is also a solid because it has a fixed shape and volume. It has physical properties such as length, color, weight, and smooth texture. The ink inside the pen is a liquid because it flows and takes the shape of its container.

services.title: Air I Breathe
services.text: Air is a gas because it does not have a fixed shape or volume. It spreads out and fills the space around it. Even though we cannot see air, it is still matter because it has mass and occupies space.

services.title: Table
services.text: A table is a solid because it keeps its shape and volume. It has physical properties such as hardness, size, weight, color, and texture. It is matter because it has mass and occupies space.

services.title: Chair
services.text: A chair is a solid object that has a fixed shape and volume. It has properties such as height, color, texture, and mass. It demonstrates matter because it occupies space and can be measured.

services.title: Ice Cube
services.text: An ice cube is a solid form of water because it has a definite shape and volume. When it melts, it becomes liquid water, showing that matter can change from one state to another.

skillData.title: My skills and interests help me as a STEM student.
skillData.text: These skills allow me to become more creative, logical, and confident in solving problems. Coding helps me create useful projects, while problem solving helps me think carefully and find better solutions.

informations.title: Why Measurement Is Important in Science
Solids, Liquids, and Gases Around Me
How Science Helps Me Understand the World
Science Reflection
informations.text: After learning about matter, measurement, atomic theory, and fundamental particles, I realized that everything around me is made up of tiny particles that are too small to see with the naked eye. I learned that matter is not only about objects we can touch, but also about the particles that make up those objects. Measurement is important because it helps us describe matter accurately using numbers and units. Atomic theory helped me understand that atoms are the building blocks of matter. I also realized that fundamental particles, such as protons, neutrons, and electrons, are important in understanding how atoms behave. This lesson made me appreciate science more because it explains many things that happen in everyday life. I now understand that even simple things like notebooks, pens, and air have scientific explanations behind them. Learning these topics showed me that science is connected to the real world. It also helped me become more curious about how matter works and how everything in the universe is formed.
